title: Polignac Numbers, Conjectures of Erdős on Gaps between Primes, Arithmetic Progressions in Primes, and the Bounded Gap Conjecture

thm:A.summary: Zhang (2013): $\liminf_n d_n \le 7 \times 10^7$. Cited as the unconditional input the paper builds on.

thm:B.summary: GPY (2009) conditional: under primes-level-of-distribution $\theta > 1/2$, $\liminf_n d_n \le C(\theta)$; under EH, $\le 16$.

thm:D.summary: GPY II (2010) quantitative: $\liminf_n d_n / [\sqrt{\log n}\,(\log\log n)^2] < \infty$.

def:1.summary: Strong Polignac number: $2k$ such that $d_n = 2k$ for infinitely many $n$. (Polignac's original conjecture is that every even $2k$ is one.)

def:2.summary: Weak Polignac number: $2k$ expressible as $p - p'$ for infinitely many prime pairs $p > p'$. (Strong implies weak; the converse is open.)

thm:1.summary: Polignac numbers have positive lower asymptotic density: $\#\{$Polignac numbers $\le N\} \ge cN$ for some explicit $c > 0$ and $N > N_0$. First quantitative attack on Polignac's conjecture.

thm:2.summary: Bounded gaps between Polignac numbers: every interval $[M, M + C']$ contains at least one Polignac number.

thm:3.summary: Limit set $J$ of $\{d_n/\log n\}$'s differences contains a full interval $[0, c]$: an Erdős conjecture establishing positive Lebesgue density of the difference-of-limit-points set.

thm:4.summary: Generalized to slowly-growing scaling $f$: for any $f \in \mathcal{F}$ with $f(n) \le \log n$ and $f \to \infty$, the set $J_f := \{d_n/f(n)\}'$ contains an interval $[0, c_f]$.

thm:5.summary: Ratio behavior: $\liminf (d_{n+1}/d_n) \cdot \log n < \infty$ and $\limsup (d_{n+1}/d_n) / \log n > 0$. Quantitative oscillation between consecutive gaps.

thm:6.summary: Existence of $d \le 7 \times 10^7$ such that arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions of primes $p$ have $p' = p + d$ as the next prime — combining Zhang's bounded gaps with Green-Tao on arithmetic progressions.

lem:1.summary: Counting lemma for admissible $k$-tuples with prescribed shift differences. Combinatorial input.

lem:2.summary: Density of Polignac-type representations within the Zhang/GPY framework.

lem:3.summary: Refinement of GPY weights to count Polignac-type events.

lem:4.summary: Bridging lemma from Zhang's bounded-gap result to bounded-gap-between-Polignac-numbers.

edges.note: Zhang's bounded-gap theorem feeds the Polignac density count.
Bounded gaps between Polignac numbers follows from positive density.
GPY II's quantitative $\sqrt{\log n}$ bound used to push limit set into $[0,c]$.
Pintz's sharper $(\log n)^{3/7}$ bound also used.

context_results.description: thm:A-E summarize the prior bounded-gaps and small-gaps results being applied. Definitions def:1, def:2 fix Polignac terminology.

polignac_density.description: thm:1 (positive lower density) + thm:2 (bounded gaps between Polignac numbers). The structural attack on Polignac's conjecture via Zhang's machinery.

limit_sets_J_J_f.description: thm:3 ($J \supset [0,c]$), thm:4 ($J_f \supset [0, c_f]$) — Erdős conjectures resolved via GPY II / Pintz quantitative liminf bounds.

ratio_and_AP_results.description: thm:5 (oscillation of $d_{n+1}/d_n$), thm:6 (APs of primes with constant gap $d \le 7\times 10^7$). Combines Zhang with Green-Tao.

technical_lemmas.description: lem:1-4 supply counting, density, and bridging arguments connecting GPY/Zhang weights to Polignac-density statements.

GPY:II.title: Primes in Tuples II
GPY:II.role: foundational — thm:D cites this; powers thm:3

Pintz:2013-earlier.title: Earlier Pintz quantitative liminf paper (referenced as [Pin2013])

GreenTao:APs.title: The primes contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions
GreenTao:APs.role: foundational — APs of primes input for thm:6

Polignac:1849.title: Recherches nouvelles sur les nombres premiers
